results.title: Paper marbling — Wikipedia
results.judge_reason: Directly describes the source-domain mechanism with carrageenan size, ox-gall surfactant, and rake/comb manipulation — exact match to source side.

results.title: Ebru — UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage
results.judge_reason: UNESCO entry on ebru describes sprinkling/brushing pigments on oily water and transferring to paper — same source-domain mechanism.

results.title: Craft of Turkish Marbling
results.judge_reason: Describes Turkish marbling techniques and named patterns (stones, peacock, getgel) — covers the source-domain craft directly.

results.title: One-Step Text Generation: Latent Capacity (2025/5)
results.judge_reason: One-step generation from compressed latent embeddings reconstructing hundreds of tokens in one forward pass closely mirrors the single-stamp single-shot extraction from accumulated latent surface.

results.title: Hardware-Aware Parallel Prompt Decoding (2024/5)
results.judge_reason: Parallel multi-token prediction in single forward pass overlaps partially with one-shot stamping but lacks the viscous-surface accumulation framing.

results.title: Parallel Text Generation Survey (2025/8)
results.judge_reason: Survey of parallel decoding and diffusion LMs covers the broader non-autoregressive space the candidate sits within but does not propose the specific viscous-surface-stamp mechanism.

results.title: Large Language Diffusion Models LLaDA (2025/2)
results.judge_reason: Diffusion LM is explicitly named in the novelty claim as different; non-autoregressive but lacks viscous accumulation + stamping framing.

results.title: Text Generation as SDE (2024/8)
results.judge_reason: SDE drift+diffusion model of autoregressive generation is acknowledged in the novelty claim as different from the proposed surface-stamp mechanism.

verdict_reason: Four results trigger hits (indices 0, 1, 2, 3). The source-domain (marbling/ebru) is well-documented in references 0-2, and reference 3 (One-Step Text Generation, arXiv:2505.21189) closely matches the LLM-side single-stamp/latent-capacity framing on multiple axes.
